name: DBeaver MCP Server
what_it_does: DBeaver MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with DBeaver's database management capabilities — connecting to multiple database types via DBeaver's JDBC driver ecosystem, executing SQL queries, browsing schema information, managing data, and leveraging DBeaver's broad database connectivity into agent-driven database administration workflows.
use_cases: Querying multiple database types through a single DBeaver connection from analytics agents
Browsing database schemas and table structures from exploration agents
Executing SQL across diverse databases (PostgreSQL, MySQL, Oracle, MSSQL) from DBA agents
Managing database connections and credentials from database admin agents
Exporting and transforming data from ETL pipeline agents
Accessing exotic databases (HBase, MongoDB, Cassandra) via DBeaver JDBC drivers
not_for: Teams without DBeaver installed (commercial DBeaver Pro for enterprise features)
High-performance database operations (DBeaver adds overhead vs direct connections)
Teams preferring direct database MCPs (PostgreSQL MCP, MySQL MCP, etc.)
best_when: An agent needs to connect to multiple heterogeneous databases through a single interface — leveraging DBeaver's extensive JDBC driver ecosystem for databases that lack native MCP servers.
avoid_when: You use a single database type — a dedicated database MCP (PostgreSQL, MySQL) is more efficient.

auth.notes: Database-specific credentials passed via DBeaver connection configuration. Each database connection uses its own auth mechanism. DBeaver manages credential storage.

pricing.free_tier_limits: DBeaver Community: free and open source
pricing.notes: DBeaver Community Edition is free. DBeaver Pro required for some advanced features. Community MCP is free open source.

performance.rate_limits: Local DBeaver — no external rate limits; database capacity bound

known_agent_gotchas: DBeaver must be running with database connections pre-configured
Community MCP — DBeaver's API is not publicly documented; may break with DBeaver updates
Connection management is in DBeaver UI — agents cannot create new connections dynamically
DBeaver Community free but adds overhead vs direct DB connections
Multiple database types supported but SQL dialects differ — agents need dialect awareness
DBeaver Pro features (NoSQL, cloud DBs) require paid license
agent_readiness.security_notes: DBeaver manages credentials. Database TLS depends on connection config. Community MCP. DBeaver must run locally. Use read-only DB users for analytics agents.

security_score_components.security_notes: DBeaver manages DB credentials. TLS per connection config. Read-only users recommended. Community MCP.
